title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Inference Brief: Direct Exchange Routing
summary: A task service publishes through the default exchange without naming the coupling, assumes one queue broadcasts, uses auto-ack workers, and calls round-robin dispatch fair despite unequal task cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the direct exchange routing increment in direct-routing work distribution service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Direct Exchange Routing · Theory Direct Exchange Routing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1206 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Direct Exchange Routing contract for the supplied data.
